preamble: The transformation of the voiced labiodental fricative [v] into the voiceless bilabial stop [p] is a form of fortition (strengthening) and devoicing, documented in specific language developments where vowels or approximants harden into full stops.

examples.note: Documented historical path where rounded vowels shifted to labial approximants and then fortified into bilabial stops.
Common devoicing of the labiodental fricative before a voiceless stop, representing a partial shift toward the voiceless labial space.

sources: Phonological research on Nakh sound laws.
